source.definition: to come into possession of something or receive something

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "get" originates from Old English "gietan", meaning to obtain or receive. It has evolved to describe the act of acquiring or coming into possession of something.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „get” provine din engleza veche „gietan”, care înseamnă a obține sau a primi. A evoluat pentru a descrie actul de a dobândi sau a intra în posesia a ceva.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used to express receiving or obtaining something, whether tangible or intangible, such as gifts or information.

target.definition: a obține sau a căpăta ceva

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a primi” derives from the Latin „recipere”, meaning to receive or obtain. It describes the act of receiving things or feelings.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a primi“ provine din latină, de la „recipere“, care înseamnă a primi sau a obține ceva.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 4th conjugation (-i). Often used to receive both material and immaterial things, such as gifts, letters, or messages.

examples.sourceText: He gets the information on time.
I will get the package tomorrow.
I would like to get it.

learningTips.text: Remember that "a primi" means "to receive" or "to get" in Romanian, similar to how you 'get' a gift in English.
Use "a primi" with different objects like gifts, messages, or help to understand its versatile use.
Think of the English word "prime" (meaning first or important) to recall "primi" as receiving something important.
Imagine you 'get' a prize or a present, which helps link the sound and meaning of "primi".
